pages.title: Processor Unit
pages.text: The silicon heart of your operations. The Processor Unit (CPU) is the primary component responsible for generating $(l:logicore:architecture/cycles)Cycles$().$(br)$(br)CPUs come in three tiers: Basic, Advance, and Ultimate. Higher tiers produce significantly more cycles per operation.

pages.title: Tier Compatibility
pages.text: CPUs can be installed in $(l:logicore:machines/server)Servers$() or $(l:logicore:machines/computer)Computers$().$(br)$(br)$(l)Server:$() Accepts all three tiers (Basic, Advance, Ultimate). Up to 9 CPUs per Server, and 9 Servers per $(l:logicore:machines/server_rack)Server Rack$().$(br)$(br)$(l)Computer:$() Accepts Basic and Advance tiers only.
The entry-level processor. Capable of producing $(1)1,000$() $(l:logicore:architecture/cycles)Cycles$() per operation. $(6)Tier: Basic$().
A mid-tier processor. Capable of producing $(2)2,500$() $(l:logicore:architecture/cycles)Cycles$() per operation. $(a)Tier: Advance$().
The pinnacle of processing technology. Capable of producing $(5)5,000$() $(l:logicore:architecture/cycles)Cycles$() per operation. $(d)Tier: Ultimate$().
